world.title: Some 450 medieval cannonballs were unearthed at a construction site in Belgium
world.description: Archaeologists uncovered around 450 medieval cannonballs at a construction site in Nieuwpoort, Belgium. The cache dates between 1350 and 1600 A.D. and may be a first-of-its-kind find.

science.title: A new force of nature is reshaping the planet, study finds
science.description: Human societies didn’t just adapt to the planet—they learned to reshape it. From early fire use to today’s global supply chains, our cultural and social innovations have unlocked extraordinary power to transform Earth and improve human life. But that progress has come with serious costs, including climate change, pollution, and mass extinction. Instead of framing this era—the Anthropocene—as pure crisis, Erle Ellis argues it’s also proof of something hopeful: when people work together, they can drive massive positive change.

science.title: In the Middle of His Farm in Patagonia, a Shepherd Found a 20-Meter Dinosaur That Doesn’t Fit Any Known Group

ai.title: 'Not AI, This Is Mizoram': Mizoram Traffic Discipline Video Wins Praises On Internet -VIDEO
ai.description: A viral video showcasing disciplined traffic behaviour in Mizoram has crossed 5 million views, sparking conversations about civic sense in India. The clip shows vehicles following lanes, avoiding honking, and waiting patiently in traffic. Netizens praised the orderliness, while locals responded modestly. The video reignited debates on how everyday behaviour shapes public spaces and road culture
